pi_items.source: Microsoft official SQL Server samples repository at https://github.com/Microsoft/sql-server-samples/releases/download/adventureworks/AdventureWorks2022.bak and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/samples/adventureworks-install-configure
Confirmed through GitHub releases page and search results
AdventureWorks2022 schema documentation confirms Production.Product table exists with columns: ProductID, Name, ProductNumber, SafetyStockLevel, ReorderPoint, and others
AdventureWorks2022 schema documentation confirms Production.ProductInventory table exists with columns: ProductID, LocationID, Quantity
AdventureWorks2022 contains Sales.SalesOrderHeader table with OrderDate column; sales data spans from 2011 to mid-2014, including the 2013-01-01 to 2014-01-01 range required by the TVF
AdventureWorks2022 contains Sales.SalesOrderDetail table with OrderQty column, used for demand calculations
Production.Product table contains a 'Name' column (nvarchar), not 'ProductName'. The view can alias this as ProductName when selecting, which is standard SQL practice
Task metadata specifies view_columns: [ProductID, ProductName, ProductNumber, TotalOnHand, SafetyStockLevel, ReorderPoint, AvgDailyDemand, EstimatedDaysOfSupply, StockoutRiskLevel]
Task description explicitly defines three risk levels with specific logical conditions in the task
AdventureWorks2022 Sales.SalesOrderHeader contains orders from 5/21/2011 to 6/30/2014, including the 2013-01-01 to 2014-01-01 range required by the task
Production.ProductInventory schema as documented in AdventureWorks2022
AdventureWorks2022 is an official Microsoft sample database, not synthetic test data. It contains realistic transactional data representing a fictional bicycle manufacturing and retail company

privileged_info_summary: AdventureWorks2022 is a real, official Microsoft SQL Server sample database available from https://github.com/Microsoft/sql-server-samples. It contains the required tables: Production.Product (with ProductID, Name, ProductNumber, SafetyStockLevel, ReorderPoint columns), Production.ProductInventory (with ProductID, LocationID, Quantity columns), and Sales.SalesOrderHeader/Detail tables. The SalesOrderHeader table contains order data spanning from May 2011 through June 2014, including the entire 2013-01-01 to 2014-01-01 range specified in the task. The Production.Product table uses the column name 'Name' (which can be aliased as 'ProductName' in SQL). All required columns for the task are present in the source tables.
